recent.title: Preston (Sole Toscana) and Pulkit Srivastava
recent.summary: Pulkit's intro call with Preston after GM transition from Mia DyBuncio. Discussed: account handover context, business performance (approaching $1M sales, 30% annual growth), primary FERMAT use case (post-click testing), best-performing funnel ('Five reasons why' landing page), testing history (AB tests, price testing), marketing channels (Meta ads + email sequences), upcoming makeup line launch, and potential NY/London relocation in June.
action_items: Pulkit to conduct deep dive analysis of account data
Share PDF analysis or schedule call to review findings
Identify new CRO opportunities and optimization tests
Monthly cadence call scheduled (continuing from Mia's cadence)
key_insights: Small skincare brand approaching $1M in sales with 30% annual growth
Preston bought the business from founders a couple years ago
All shop links redirect to FERMAT pages for better performance
Very satisfied with service — switched from bi-weekly to monthly check-ins
Low-touch account requiring minimal oversight but regular performance reviews
Product expansion: makeup line launch planned for later in the year

calls.analysis: One confirmed call in the period: Mar 10 intro call between Pulkit and Preston (Sole Toscana), verified via Granola meeting notes (ID: 496a8a00), Glyphic summary email, and Zoom join notification. Glyphic API returned persistent 500 errors during this refresh — call recording/transcript not retrievable via API. Granola provided full meeting content. Account is categorized as low-touch with monthly cadence.

slack.analysis: Sole Toscana's Slack channel (#fermat-x-sole-toscana) shows a clear GM transition on Mar 4, 2026 — Mia DyBuncio introduced Pulkit Srivastava as the new Growth Manager. Preston (brand contact) responded the same day by scheduling a call. Since then, the channel has been silent for 33 days (no messages after Mar 4). Pierre weekly roundups stopped posting after Jan 12, suggesting a configuration issue — the last roundup showed 2,180 sessions and 54 purchases for Jan 5-11. Historical data shows strong Black Friday/Cyber Monday performance (224 purchases, $34,838 GMV for Nov 24-30). Key channel topics: Klaviyo integration steps (Nov 18), AI Search product launch (Nov 13), and holiday coverage (Dec 23).
